meaning: n.假象,伪装,假装,假托,佯攻,虚击vi.装作,假装,佯攻adj.假的,虚饰的,不鲜明的,淡的
examples: It is only a feint, comfort for us.
Is this just a feint to unnerve the neighbours (there was similar activity in April 2005, but no test)?
But are they steps in the right direction, or a feint by Iran to dodge curbs on its suspect ambitions?
Last October, the company made a multimillion-dollar investment in mobile games developer Aurora Feint.
Unless it was a cunning political feint, Mr Erdogan's anti-EU outburst is thus hard to understand.
